task: Implement a JavaScript function `semverCompare(a, b)` that compares two Semantic Versioning 2.0.0 version strings per semver.org §11 and returns `-1` when a has lower precedence than b, `1` when a has higher precedence, and `0` when they are of equal precedence. Parse each version as `major.minor.patch[-prerelease][+build]`. Rules, applied in order: (1) Compare major, then minor, then patch as NUMBERS (so 1.0.2 < 1.0.10 and 1.2.0 < 1.10.0 — never a string compare). (2) When the major.minor.patch core is equal, a version WITH a pre-release has LOWER precedence than one WITHOUT a pre-release (`1.0.0-alpha` < `1.0.0`). (3) When both carry a pre-release, split each on `.` and compare identifiers left to right: identifiers that are all digits compare NUMERICALLY; identifiers with letters compare by ASCII lexical order; a purely numeric identifier ALWAYS has lower precedence than an alphanumeric one (so `1.0.0-999` < `1.0.0-alpha`); and if every compared identifier is equal, the version with MORE pre-release fields has higher precedence (`1.0.0-alpha` < `1.0.0-alpha.1`). (4) Build metadata (everything from a `+`) is IGNORED for precedence, so `1.0.0+build` compares EQUAL to `1.0.0` (return 0). The canonical ascending chain each adjacent pair of which must compare to -1 is: `1.0.0-1` < `1.0.0-alpha` < `1.0.0-alpha.1` < `1.0.0-alpha.beta` < `1.0.0-beta` < `1.0.0-beta.2` < `1.0.0-beta.11` < `1.0.0-rc.1` < `1.0.0` < `1.0.1` < `1.1.0` < `2.0.0`. These are adversarial cases: numeric-vs-lexical core comparison (`1.0.0-beta.2` < `1.0.0-beta.11`), pre-release-below-release, numeric-identifier-below-alpha, more-fields-wins, and build-metadata-ignored — exactly where a naive string compare, a forgotten pre-release rule, or a lexical numeric compare diverges. Comparisons must be symmetric: swapping the arguments negates the result. Return ONLY the function source as text. Do NOT create or write any files.
visibleSpec: semverCompare(a, b) -> -1 | 0 | 1. Compares two SemVer 2.0.0 strings by precedence. major.minor.patch compared numerically; a pre-release version is lower than the same core without one; pre-release identifiers compared dot-by-dot (numeric numerically, alphanumeric by ASCII, numeric < alphanumeric, more fields wins on equal prefix); build metadata (+...) is ignored so 1.0.0+x equals 1.0.0. Returns -1 if a<b, 1 if a>b, 0 if equal precedence; symmetric.
scope: Single pure function returning -1, 0, or 1. No mutation of the inputs, no extra parameters, no helper globals, no I/O, no external libraries. Inputs are well-formed semver strings.

hidden_tests.name: chain alpha vs alpha.1 more fields
build on both, different meta, equal
numeric pre always below alpha pre
